title: The hidden cost of always being available
summary: Argue the harm caused by the modern habit of being reachable at every hour.
topic: The hidden cost of always being available
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) arguing what we lose by being constantly reachable through phones and messaging apps. Use a specific moment as evidence. Use the second conditional, present perfect, and modals.
hintStarters: The phone in my pocket has changed…
If I switched it off for an evening,…
What worries me is not the screen but…
sampleText:
The phone in my pocket has changed the shape of my attention, and I have only really noticed since I started keeping a small notebook at dinner. Last spring, I sat down with my partner at a restaurant in Athens, ordered a salad, and tried to count how many times I checked my phone before the food arrived. The answer was eleven. None of the messages mattered. One was a colleague forwarding a meme; one was a delivery confirmation for a parcel that wasn't even mine. The salad was excellent. I have almost no memory of it.

What worries me is not the screen but the soft promise it carries: that I will reply. If I switched it off for an evening, nothing terrible would happen at work, and most of my friends would forgive a six-hour delay. However, I have built a quiet contract with everyone in my address book — colleagues, neighbours, my mother, the man who fixes my bike — that I am always one minute away. The contract has costs nobody put in writing. I notice slower. I finish fewer thoughts. I have started reading a single page three times because halfway down it I check the screen out of habit. The phone is not the whole problem; the expectation is. Even when it is dark, I behave as if it might light up.

Some people will say that being reachable is a kindness, that responsible adults answer their messages. They are partly right, and I'd never argue for vanishing for days. However, between disappearing and being on call, there is a wide middle that most of us have stopped using. We could agree, as a workplace or a family, that no one expects a reply between dinner and breakfast, or for two hours every Saturday afternoon. We could write that down. We could defend it. If we don't, the slow cost will be exactly what it has been for me — meals I half-tasted, evenings I half-spent, conversations I half-followed. Those halves add up to a life that feels busy but oddly thin. The phone deserves a smaller chair at the table, not the head of it.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hình dáng sự chú ý của tôi
một cuốn sổ nhỏ trong bữa tối
trước khi đồ ăn được mang đến
lời hứa nhẹ nhàng nó mang theo
những bữa ăn tôi chỉ nếm được một nửa
bận rộn nhưng lạ lùng hời hợt

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Second conditional for a soft argument
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'If I switched it off for an evening, nothing terrible would happen at work' dùng câu điều kiện loại 2 để diễn đạt một tình huống giả định và hậu quả nhẹ nhàng.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'If I switched it off for an evening, nothing terrible would happen at work' uses the second conditional to express a hypothetical situation and its mild consequence.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Modal 'could' for proposal
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'We could agree, as a workplace or a family, that no one expects a reply…' dùng 'could' để gợi ý mềm, không áp đặt.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'We could agree, as a workplace or a family, that no one expects a reply…' uses 'could' to propose a soft suggestion without imposing.

sampleTranslationVi:
Chiếc điện thoại trong túi tôi đã thay đổi hình dáng sự chú ý của tôi, và tôi chỉ thực sự nhận ra điều đó từ khi tôi bắt đầu mang một cuốn sổ nhỏ trong bữa tối. Mùa xuân năm ngoái, tôi ngồi xuống với người yêu tại một nhà hàng ở Athens, gọi một đĩa salad, và cố đếm xem tôi đã kiểm tra điện thoại bao nhiêu lần trước khi đồ ăn được mang đến. Câu trả lời là mười một. Không tin nào quan trọng cả. Một là đồng nghiệp chuyển tiếp một meme; một là xác nhận giao hàng cho một gói hàng thậm chí không phải của tôi. Đĩa salad rất ngon. Tôi gần như không nhớ gì về nó.

Điều làm tôi lo không phải màn hình mà là lời hứa nhẹ nhàng nó mang theo: rằng tôi sẽ trả lời. Nếu tôi tắt nó trong một buổi tối, không có gì khủng khiếp sẽ xảy ra ở công ty, và hầu hết bạn bè tôi sẽ tha thứ sự chậm trễ sáu tiếng. Tuy nhiên, tôi đã xây dựng một hợp đồng thầm lặng với mọi người trong danh bạ của mình — đồng nghiệp, hàng xóm, mẹ tôi, người sửa xe đạp cho tôi — rằng tôi luôn cách họ một phút. Hợp đồng có những cái giá không ai viết ra. Tôi chú ý chậm hơn. Tôi hoàn thành ít suy nghĩ hơn. Tôi đã bắt đầu đọc một trang sách ba lần vì đến nửa trang tôi lại kiểm tra màn hình theo thói quen. Điện thoại không phải toàn bộ vấn đề; kỳ vọng mới là. Ngay cả khi nó tối, tôi vẫn cư xử như thể nó có thể sáng lên.

Một số người sẽ nói rằng việc luôn có mặt là một sự tử tế, rằng người lớn có trách nhiệm phải trả lời tin nhắn. Họ đúng một phần, và tôi sẽ không bao giờ ủng hộ việc biến mất hàng ngày. Tuy nhiên, giữa biến mất và trực sẵn sàng, có một khoảng giữa rộng mà phần lớn chúng ta đã ngừng sử dụng. Chúng ta có thể đồng ý, với tư cách một nơi làm việc hay một gia đình, rằng không ai mong đợi trả lời giữa bữa tối và bữa sáng, hay trong hai tiếng mỗi chiều thứ Bảy. Chúng ta có thể viết điều đó ra. Chúng ta có thể bảo vệ nó. Nếu chúng ta không làm thế, cái giá chậm rãi sẽ chính là điều đã xảy ra với tôi — những bữa ăn chỉ nếm được một nửa, những buổi tối chỉ trải nghiệm một nửa, những cuộc trò chuyện chỉ theo dõi một nửa. Những nửa đó cộng lại thành một cuộc đời cảm thấy bận rộn nhưng lạ lùng hời hợt. Chiếc điện thoại xứng đáng một chiếc ghế nhỏ hơn ở bàn, không phải đầu bàn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the second conditional correctly?
options: If I switched it off for an evening, nothing terrible would happen at work.
If I switch it off for an evening, nothing terrible would happen at work.
If I switched it off for an evening, nothing terrible will happen at work.
If I would switch it off for an evening, nothing terrible would happen at work.
mcQuestions.explanation: Second conditional = past simple after 'if' + 'would' + base verb in the main clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the present perfect that fits a recent realisation.
options: I am only really noticed since I started keeping a small notebook at dinner.
I only really noticed since I am started keeping a small notebook at dinner.
I have only really noticed since I started keeping a small notebook at dinner.
I have only really notice since I start keeping a small notebook at dinner.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect ('have noticed') + 'since' + past simple anchors a present awareness to a past starting point.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses 'could' correctly to propose a soft change?
options: We could agree, as a workplace or a family, that no one expects a reply.
We could to agree, as a workplace or a family, that no one expects a reply.
We could agreed, as a workplace or a family, that no one expects a reply.
We can could agree, as a workplace or a family, that no one expects a reply.
mcQuestions.explanation: Modal verbs are followed directly by the base verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence has the correct preposition?
options: I check the screen out of habit.
I check the screen by of habit.
I check the screen on of habit.
I check the screen from of habit.
mcQuestions.explanation: The fixed phrase is 'out of habit'.

options: However, between disappearing and being on call, there is a wide middle that most of us have stopped using.
However that between disappearing and being on call, a wide middle most of us have stopped using.
Between disappearing however on call being, there is a wide middle.
However is a wide middle between disappearing being on call most of us stopped using.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'However' starts a sentence with a comma; the rest follows as a full clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence at B1 register.
options: Even when it is dark, I behave as if it might light up.
Even when it is darkness, I'm behaving like it might to light up.
Even is dark when, I behave as if light up it might.
Even when dark it is, I behaviour as light it might up.
mcQuestions.explanation: Standard SVO with 'as if + might + base verb' is the natural B1 form.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present perfect correctly to express a habit started in the past?
options: I have started reading a single page three times because halfway down it I check the screen out of habit.
I have start reading a single page three times.
I starting have read a single page three times.
I am have started reading a single page three times.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Have started + verb-ing' is correct; the others are ungrammatical.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence handles an objection naturally?
options: Some people will say that being reachable is a kindness. They are partly right, and I'd never argue for vanishing for days.
Some people, reachable kindness will say. Partly right are they.
Reachable being is a kindness, will some people say. Right partly they are.
Some people will say reachable is kindness, however, I never argue vanish.
mcQuestions.explanation: Cleanly state the objection in one sentence and acknowledge it in the next.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses parallel structure correctly?
options: Meals I half-tasted, evenings I half-spent, conversations I half-followed.
Meals half-tasted, evenings half-spending, conversations half-followed.
I half-tasted meals, evenings half-spent of me, conversations followed half.
Half-tasting meals, half-spent evenings, half-follow conversations.
mcQuestions.explanation: Three parallel noun phrases of the same shape: noun + 'I half-Xed'.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the grammatical shape of each item in the list.

options: Even when it is dark, I behave as if it might light up.
Even when it is dark, I behave as if it might lights up.
Even when it is dark, I behave as if it might to light up.
Even when it is dark, I behave as if it might lit up.
mcQuestions.explanation: Modal + base verb is the only correct structure.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If a workplace formally banned messages between 8 p.m. and 7 a.m., would productivity rise, fall, or stay roughly the same — and what would change first?
